text:
There once was a young orphan girl named Olive who lived in a tiny cottage by a forested clearing. Each evening at sunset, Olive would look out at the shimmering lake nearby. One day, she noticed a small bird that seemed lost.

"You're not lost," Olive said cautiously. The bird spread its wings. It was Patch!

"Why didn't you ask for directions?" Patch asked sadly.

"It's hard talking to birds," Olive confessed. "You don't understand me."

In the days that followed, Olive would chat with Patch every evening. They shared delicious berries.

One day, Olive realized she could talk to animals. Birds. Dogs. Maybe pigeons?

Patch was fascinated. Every bird wanted to know the secret.

"Well," Olive said, puffing her chest out. "If I tell you, just promise me... you'll be silent about it."

You'll what?"

"You'll never tell anyone."

"Why?" she asked hopefully.

Because once you say it...

All birds
